name: semver
usage_summary: Used in two cli-kit source files: node-package-manager.ts imports SemVer (class for comparison via .compare()) and semverSatisfies (range check for package version requirements); version.ts imports satisfies to gate global CLI usage on versions >=3.59.0. Both usages involve semantic version parsing, comparison, and range satisfaction checks.
how_to_remove: Node.js does not have a built-in semver parser, but the usage is limited to three call sites: two SemVer.compare() calls and two satisfies(version, range) calls. These can be replaced with the lightweight `compare-versions` package (zero-dependency, ~800 bytes), or inlined with a simple regex-based comparator. The range syntax used is simple (>=x.y.z) so a custom implementation would be trivial. Estimated effort: ~20–40 lines or a swap to compare-versions.
replacement_reason: Only three call sites using basic comparison and a simple range check. compare-versions covers all these cases with zero transitive dependencies and a much smaller footprint than the full semver package.
